customer_anxiety_profile: Robert is financially cautious and time-conscious. His primary concern is cost — specifically that the solar payment not exceed his current electric bill and that there be no upfront installation cost. He raised this concern three times across the call in escalating specificity. He is not hostile but has a low tolerance for back-and-forth and will disengage if the appointment feels like a sales pitch rather than a direct information session.
unresolved_concerns: Long-run cost: Robert asked multiple times what he will end up paying over time and never received a specific answer.
Transferability: Robert asked whether the system is transferable if they move — acknowledged by Holland but not answered.
Bill comparison: Robert's explicit condition for proceeding is that the solar payment not be higher than his current electric bill — this was never anchored to an actual dollar figure.
Roof leak protection: Robert asked about leak protection after installation — Holland confirmed warranties exist but gave no specifics.
sensitive_topics_raised: Wife's absence from the appointment: Robert disclosed his wife goes to work Wednesdays and will not be on the call. She plans to review paperwork separately. The closer should be aware the co-decision-maker is not present and should plan accordingly.
Robert's time sensitivity: Robert explicitly said he does not want 'a whole lot of back and forth' and expects the appointment to be direct and under 30 minutes.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment scheduled for Wednesday at 2:30 PM.
Text sent with appointment details for calendar.
Text sent requesting photo of paper utility bill (Dominion) showing 12-month kilowatt usage bar graph.
Roof leak warranty coverage confirmed ('if you did happen to get a leak or anything, then we'd cover that').
No money upfront or out of pocket confirmed.
closer_should_know: The wife is not on this appointment and plans to review paperwork separately — she is on the title or at minimum a co-decision-maker per Robert's own framing ('takes both of us'). The closer should address this at the top of the call and either include her or explicitly plan a follow-up step that brings her in before any commitment is made. Robert's financial anxiety is unresolved and specific: he needs to see that the solar payment is lower than his current bill before he will move forward.
